headlines.description: Brighton & Hove Albion were held to a 1-1 draw by Fulham in their Premier League opener at the American Express Stadium on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Muniz late show secures Fulham point at Brighton

headlines.description: Sunderland marked their return to the Premier League in style with a 3-0 win over West Ham at the Stadium of Light.
headlines.shortLinkText: Sunderland mark PL return with win vs. West Ham

headlines.description: Tottenham Hotspur began the season with a 3-0 win over Burnley in north London on Saturday as Thomas Frank got his tenure off to the perfect start.
headlines.shortLinkText: Frank off to flyer as Spurs swat aside Burnley

headlines.description: Manchester City began their quest for a seventh Premier League title under Pep Guardiola with a statement 4-0 win over Wolves at the Molineux.
headlines.shortLinkText: Haaland, Reijnders lead Man City to statement win

headlines.description: Chelsea began the season with a 0-0 draw with Crystal Palace at Stamford Bridge to begin the new Premier League season.
headlines.shortLinkText: VAR disallows Eze goal in Chelsea-Palace draw

headlines.description: A brace from Chris Wood led Nottingham Forest to a comfortable 3-1 win against Brentford, despite a late consolation from Thiago.
headlines.shortLinkText: Wood fires Forest to dominant win over Brentford

headlines.description: Arsenal got their Premier League title tilt off to a winning start as they earned a narrow win over Ruben Amorim's new-look Manchester United team.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal set-piece secures win over new-look Utd

headlines.description: Jack Grealish was unable to inspire Everton after going on as a second-half substitute for his debut in a 1-0 loss to promoted Leeds United in the Premier League on Monday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Leeds win Prem return in Grealish's Everton debut

headlines.description: Chelsea shrugged off the absence of Cole Palmer to get their season up and running in style while deepening the gloom at West Ham.
headlines.shortLinkText: Newcomers star as Chelsea thrash sorry West Ham

headlines.description: Tottenham earned a 2-0 away win over Manchester City on Saturday, maintaining their top spot in the Premier League.
headlines.shortLinkText: Tottenham shine in 2-0 win over disjointed Man City

headlines.description: Marcus Tavernier's early deflected finish earned Bournemouth a 1-0 Premier League win over 10-man Wolves at Vitality Stadium.
headlines.shortLinkText: Tavernier goals helps Bournemouth edge 10-man Wolves

headlines.description: Dango Ouattara's debut goal saw Brentford defeat Aston Villa 1-0 and earn Keith Andrews his first Premier League win as manager.
headlines.shortLinkText: Ouattara debut goal sees Brentford past Villa

headlines.description: Burnley got their Premier League campaign up and running with a 2-0 win over Sunderland at Turf Moor.
headlines.shortLinkText: Burnley kickstart Premier League campaign with Sunderland win

headlines.description: Viktor Gyökeres fired home his first Arsenal goals in a thumping 5-0 Premier League victory over Leeds at the Emirates.
headlines.shortLinkText: Gyökeres scores two as Arsenal thrash Leeds

headlines.description: Crystal Palace and Nottingham Forest held each other to a 1-1 draw in a game that was billed as the "battle of the boardrooms."
headlines.shortLinkText: Hudson-Odoi leveller seals Forest, Palace draw

headlines.description: Iliman Ndiaye and James Garner got Everton up and running at the Hill Dickinson Stadium in a 2-0 win over Brighton.
headlines.shortLinkText: Grealish stars in Everton debut win at new home

headlines.description: Fulham substitute Emile Smith Rowe's strike held Manchester United to a 1-1 draw at Craven Cottage on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man United still looking for win after 1-1 draw

headlines.description: Newcastle United and Liverpool went head-to-head in a feisty clash at St James' Park, which saw the Premier League champions earn a last-gasp 3-2 win with a goal from 16-year-old Rio Ngumoha.
headlines.shortLinkText: Liverpool's Ngumoha, 16, stuns Newcastle late

headlines.description: A Joao Pedro header and an Enzo Fernandez penalty helped Chelsea get the better of Fulham in a match dominated by interventions from the VAR.
headlines.shortLinkText: Controversial VAR calls help Chelsea beat Fulham

headlines.description: A last-gasp Bruno Fernandes penalty rescued three points for Manchester United against Burnley as Ruben Amorim's side secured their first Premier League win of the season in dramatic fashion.
headlines.shortLinkText: 97th-min Fernandes penalty rescues United win

headlines.description: Wilson Isidor scored a last-gasp header as Sunderland came from behind to beat Brentford 2-1 at the Stadium of Light.
headlines.shortLinkText: Late Isidior goal gives Sunderland win vs Brentford

headlines.description: Evanilson's fifth-minute goal earned Bournemouth a deserved 1-0 victory at Tottenham and brought Thomas Frank's side back down to earth.
headlines.shortLinkText: Dire Spurs dominated by Bournemouth in 1-0 loss

headlines.description: Jack Grealish continued his excellent start to life at Everton with two more assists in a 3-2 win over troubled Wolves.
headlines.shortLinkText: Grealish shines again as Everton hold off Wolves

headlines.description: Leeds and Newcastle shared the points in a Premier League stalemate at Elland Road on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Isak missing as Newcastle labour to draw at Leeds

name: Manchester City at Brighton & Hove Albion

headlines.description: Brajan Gruda rolled home a dramatic late winner as Brighton roared back to stun Manchester City with a thrilling 2-1 victory at the Amex Stadium.
headlines.shortLinkText: City lose again as Brighton dent Pep's title tilt

name: West Ham United at Nottingham Forest

headlines.description: West Ham eased the pressure on Graham Potter as three late goals saw them move off the foot of the League table with an extraordinary 3-0 victory at Forest.
headlines.shortLinkText: West Ham score 3 late at Forest to earn 1st win

headlines.description: Liverpool got an early leg up in the Premier League title race as Dominik Szoboszlai's jaw-dropping free kick secured a 1-0 victory over Arsenal at Anfield on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Szoboszlai stunner sees Liverpool edge Arsenal

headlines.description: Marc Guehi scored a stunner on what could be his Crystal Palace farewell as the Eagles thrashed goal-shy Aston Villa 3-0 on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Guéhi scores stunner as Palace thrash Villa 3-0

headlines.description: Two goals from Martín Zubimendi either side of a Viktor Gyökeres strike saw Arsenal's summer signings condemn Nottingham Forest to a 3-0 defeat.
headlines.shortLinkText: Zubimendi brace sees Ange beaten in Forest bow

name: Brighton & Hove Albion at AFC Bournemouth

headlines.description: Antoine Semenyo scored one goal and set up the other as Bournemouth beat Brighton 2-1 in the Premier League.
headlines.shortLinkText: Semenyo stars as Bournemouth win 3 in a row

headlines.description: Yeremy Pino squandered a golden chance to mark his first Premier League start with the winner as Crystal Palace were held to a goalless draw by Sunderland.
headlines.shortLinkText: Palace frustrated in goalless draw with Sunderland

headlines.description: Aston Villa's search for a win continued as a goalless draw at Everton did nothing to improve their sorry start to the Premier League season.
headlines.shortLinkText: Goalless draw hands Villa worst start since '97

headlines.description: A bright cameo by new signing Kevin inspired Fulham to a first Premier League win of the season with Gabriel Gudmundsson's 94th-minute own goal enough to clinch a 1-0 victory over Leeds.
headlines.shortLinkText: Last-minute own goal sees Fulham beat Leeds

headlines.description: Nick Woltemade paid off the first instalment of his £69 million transfer fee as he headed Newcastle to a first Premier League win of the season on his debut.
headlines.shortLinkText: Woltemade nets on debut as Toon win without Isak

name: Tottenham Hotspur at West Ham United

headlines.description: Graham Potter found himself back under pressure as Tottenham returned to winning ways with a 3-0 London derby demolition of West Ham.
headlines.shortLinkText: Spurs rout West Ham to pile pressure on Potter

headlines.description: Fábio Carvalho scored in the third minute of added time as Brentford rescued a dramatic 2-2 draw against Chelsea at the Gtech Community Stadium.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brentford deny Chelsea with stoppage-time goal

headlines.description: Mohamed Salah's penalty in the final minute of added time saw Liverpool produce yet more late heroics to snatch a hard-earned 1-0 win at Burnley.
headlines.shortLinkText: Last-gasp pen maintains Liverpool's perfect start

headlines.description: Erling Haaland's brace helped secure Manchester City a 3-0 derby victory against Manchester United on a day when the rivals remembered Ricky Hatton.
headlines.shortLinkText: City outclass Utd in emotional Manchester derby

headlines.description: Liverpool maintained their perfect start to the Premier League season with a narrow 2-1 victory over Everton in Saturday's Merseyside derby.
headlines.shortLinkText: Liverpool battle to derby win, maintain 100% start

name: Tottenham Hotspur at Brighton & Hove Albion

headlines.description: Brighton defender Jan Paul van Hecke scored a late own goal as Tottenham battled back from two goals down to salvage a 2-2 Premier League draw at the Amex Stadium.
headlines.shortLinkText: Late own goal hands Spurs draw at Brighton

headlines.description: Ange Postecoglou secured his first Premier League point as Nottingham Forest manager in a 1-1 draw at Burnley.
headlines.shortLinkText: Ange still waiting for win as Burnley hold Forest

name: Crystal Palace at West Ham United

headlines.description: Graham Potter prospects of keeping his job as West Ham manager are looking ever more bleak after Crystal Palace won 2-1 at a mutinous London Stadium.
headlines.shortLinkText: Potter pressure mounts as West Ham lose to Palace

headlines.description: Leeds came from behind to beat Wolves 3-1 and extend the hosts' losing start to the Premier League season to five matches.
headlines.shortLinkText: Stach stunner helps Leeds beat winless Wolves

headlines.description: Manchester United scored a much-needed 2-1 win over Chelsea in a chaotic battle that saw both teams reduced to 10 men amid torrential rain at Old Trafford.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man Utd hold off Chelsea in rainy battle of 10 men

headlines.description: Fulham stung Brentford with two rapid first-half goals to fight back and win 3-1 against their west London rivals at Craven Cottage.
headlines.shortLinkText: Fulham fight back to win derby with Brentford

headlines.description: Newcastle boss Eddie Howe's wait for a win against old club Bournemouth goes on after a drab 0-0 on the south coast.
headlines.shortLinkText: Bournemouth, Newcastle play out 0-0 bore draw

headlines.description: Aston Villa are still on the hunt for their first Premier League win of the season after being held to a 1-1 draw by 10-man Sunderland at the Stadium of Light.
headlines.shortLinkText: Villa finally score, but held by 10-man Sunderland

headlines.description: Gabriel Martinelli netted a stoppage-time equaliser to rescue a point for Arsenal against Manchester City in a fiery Premier League game at the Emirates Stadium on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Super-sub Martinelli rescues Arsenal draw vs. City

headlines.description: Manchester United slumped to yet another Premier League defeat on Saturday after a slow start saw them beaten 3-1 away to Brentford.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brentford dominate Utd, hand Amorim latest loss

headlines.description: Brighton struck twice in second-half stoppage-time as 10-man Chelsea threw away the lead to slump to a 3-1 Premier League defeat at Stamford Bridge.
headlines.shortLinkText: Welbeck nets twice to down 10-man Chelsea

headlines.description: Liverpool were given a taste of their own medicine by Crystal Palace as Eddie Nketiah's last-gasp winner wrecked their 100% start to the season.
headlines.shortLinkText: Late Nketiah winner hands Liverpool first defeat

headlines.description: Eli Kroupi struck a stoppage-time equaliser to rescue a point for Bournemouth in a 2-2 draw at Leeds.
headlines.shortLinkText: Late strike rescues point for Bournemouth at Leeds

headlines.description: Two Maxime Estève own goals and a late Erling Haaland brace helped Manchester City beat Burnley 5-1 to make it 14 wins in a row over the Clarets.
headlines.shortLinkText: Haaland double gives City huge win over Burnley

headlines.description: Sunderland clinched their first away win of the season with a 1-0 victory over Nottingham Forest in Ange Postecoglou's first home game in charge.
headlines.shortLinkText: Sunderland beat Forest to ruin Ange's home debut

headlines.description: João Palhinha rescued a 1-1 draw for Tottenham Hotspur with a stoppage-time leveller to deny Wolves a first Premier League victory of the season.
headlines.shortLinkText: Late Palhinha equaliser rescues toothless Spurs

headlines.description: Aston Villa came from a goal down to beat Fulham 3-1 at Villa Park as they picked up their first Premier League victory of the season.
headlines.shortLinkText: Villa finally off the mark with big win over Fulham

headlines.description: A last-gasp winner from Gabriel Magalhães helped Arsenal to earn a stunning 2-1 comeback victory over Newcastle United at St. James' Park on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal edge Newcastle with late Gabriel winner

headlines.description: Jarrod Bowen's deflected strike helped Nuno Espirito Santo start his reign as West Ham boss with a battling 1-1 draw at Everton.
headlines.shortLinkText: Nuno begins West Ham reign with point at Everton

headlines.description: Antoine Semenyo's double and a stunning strike from Justin Kluivert saw Bournemouth soar to second in the Premier League courtesy of 3-1 comeback win over Fulham at a rain-soaked Vitality Stadium.
headlines.shortLinkText: Semenyo downs Fulham to send Bournemouth second

headlines.description: Mohammed Kudus opened his account for his new club as Tottenham beat Leeds at Elland Road to move up to second in the Premier League.
headlines.shortLinkText: Spurs win before break for 1st time since Nov. '23

headlines.description: Declan Rice scored against his former club and Bukayo Saka converted from the penalty spot as Arsenal comfortably dispatched West Ham in the Premier League.
headlines.shortLinkText: Rice haunts West Ham in comfortable Arsenal win

headlines.description: Manchester United registered a victory over Sunderland that will allow head coach Ruben Amorim to sleep a little easier during the international break.
headlines.shortLinkText: Relief for Amorim as Utd earn much-needed win

headlines.description: Chelsea's teenage star Estêvão sparked wild scenes at Stamford Bridge as his stoppage-time goal inflicted Liverpool's third consecutive defeat.
headlines.shortLinkText: Late Estêvão goal extends Liverpool's losing streak

headlines.description: Donyell Malen's two goals continued Aston Villa's revival as they beat Burnley 2-1 to make it four wins in a row.
headlines.shortLinkText: Malen brace sees Aston Villa beat Burnley

headlines.description: Jack Grealish's first goal for Everton snatched a 2-1 victory in added time to end Crystal Palace's 19-game unbeaten run.
headlines.shortLinkText: Grealish ends Palace's 19-game unbeaten run

headlines.description: Newcastle United became the latest team to get the better of Ange Postegoclou's Nottingham Forest as they registered a 2-0 win at St. James' Park.
headlines.shortLinkText: Pressure piles on Ange as Forest lose at Newcastle

name: Brighton & Hove Albion at Wolverhampton Wanderers

headlines.description: Erling Haaland's eighth goal of the season was enough to earn Manchester City all three points form their Premier League clash at Brenford on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Haaland on target again as City beat Brentford

headlines.description: Ange Postecoglou's Nottingham Forest reign was brought to an abrupt end on Saturday as he was sacked just 19 minutes after the team's defeat to Chelsea.
headlines.shortLinkText: Ange sacked after Chelsea deliver latest defeat

name: Newcastle United at Brighton & Hove Albion

headlines.description: Danny Welbeck scored a brace as Brighton recorded a 2-1 Premier League victory over Newcastle at the Amex Stadium.
headlines.shortLinkText: Woltemade magic thwarted as Brighton strike late

headlines.description: Loum Tchaouna struck a second-half stunner as Burnley beat Leeds 2-0 in the Premier League's battle between two newly-promoted sides at Turf Moor.
headlines.shortLinkText: Tchaouna stunner seals win for Burnley vs. Leeds

headlines.description: Mateta scored a hat trick, including a stoppage-time penalty, but he will still be thinking about the one that got away after Palace drew with Bournemouth.
headlines.shortLinkText: Mateta nets 3 but spurns late chance in thriller

headlines.description: Erling Haaland continued his blistering start to the season as his quickfire second-half double propelled Manchester City to a 2-0 win over Everton.
headlines.shortLinkText: Haaland fires City to comfortable win over Everton

headlines.description: Sunderland edged to their fourth Premier League win of the season with a 2-0 victory over strugglers Wolves.
headlines.shortLinkText: Sunderland notch 4th PL win as Wolves lose again

headlines.description: Arsenal extended their lead at the top of the Premier League to three points after Leandro Trossard's goal fired Arteta's side to victory at Fulham.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal edge Fulham to move clear at top of PL

headlines.description: Morgan Rogers and Emi Buendía were on target for Aston Villa in a come-from-behind victory over Tottenham Hotspur.
headlines.shortLinkText: Buendía strike gives Villa crucial win at Spurs

headlines.description: Harry Maguire's late header secured Manchester United's first win at Anfield in almost a decade.
headlines.shortLinkText: Maguire's late goal hands Liverpool 4th straight loss

headlines.description: West Ham fans picked the right night to stage a boycott as their side slumped to a dispiriting 2-0 defeat at home to Brentford.
headlines.shortLinkText: West Ham woes continue after home loss to Brentford

name: West Ham United at Leeds United

headlines.description: Brenden Aaronson and Joe Rodon scored early goals as Leeds beat West Ham 2-1 at Elland Road to heap more misery on the Londoners.
headlines.shortLinkText: USMNT's Aaronson scores as Leeds beat West Ham

headlines.description: Chemsdine Talbi struck a dramatic stoppage-time winner as Sunderland stunned Chelsea with a 2-1 victory at Stamford Bridge.
headlines.shortLinkText: Sunderland edge Chelsea with late Talbi strike

headlines.description: Bruno Guimarães scored a last-gasp winner as Newcastle United left it late to beat Fulham at St. James' Park.
headlines.shortLinkText: Guimarães strikes late as Newcastle beat Fulham

name: Brighton & Hove Albion at Manchester United

headlines.description: Manchester United notched a third-straight victory in the Premier League on Saturday with a 3-2 win over Brighton at Old Trafford.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man United beat Brighton for third-straight win

headlines.description: Liverpool matched their worst-ever Premier League run as they sunk to a 3-2 defeat at Brentford on Saturday for their fourth consecutive league loss.
headlines.shortLinkText: Liverpool lose again to equal worst-ever Prem run

headlines.description: Sean Dyche lost his first league game as Forest boss as Bournemouth scored twice in the first half.
headlines.shortLinkText: Forest woes continue as Dyche loses first PL game

headlines.description: A first-half goal from Eberechi Eze was enough to separate Arsenal and his former club Crystal Palace.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal edge Palace as Eze goal haunts ex-club

headlines.description: Matty Cash's brilliant first-half goal earned Aston Villa an excellent 1-0 win over Manchester City, whose Premier League title hopes took an early blow.
headlines.shortLinkText: Matty Cash belter sees Aston Villa upset Man City

headlines.description: Lyle Foster came off the bench and scored a dramatic last-minute winner as Burnley snatched a 3-2 Premier League victory away to bottom side Wolves.
headlines.shortLinkText: Foster last gasp goal heaps misery on Wolves

headlines.description: Captain Micky van de Ven led from the front with two headers to inflict Everton's first defeat at their new stadium as Tottenham moved into third with a 3-0 victory.
headlines.shortLinkText: Van de Ven nets twice as Spurs overpower Everton

name: Leeds United at Brighton & Hove Albion

headlines.description: Diego Gómez scored his first Premier League goals of the season as Brighton romped to a 3-0 win over a desperately poor Leeds at the Amex Stadium.
headlines.shortLinkText: Gomez at the double as Brighton beat Leeds

headlines.description: Viktor Gyökeres and Declan Rice struck first-half goals as Arsenal won 2-0 at Burnley to extend their lead at the Premier League's summit and make it nine straight wins in all competitions.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal cleansheet run continues in Burnley win

headlines.description: Jean-Philippe Mateta's eighth goal of the season set Crystal Palace on course for a 2-0 victory over Brentford at Selhurst Park that got them back to winning ways in the Premier League.
headlines.shortLinkText: Mateta on target as Crystal Palace beat Brentford

headlines.description: Winless Wolves slumped to a 3-0 defeat at Fulham to cast huge doubt over the future of boss Vítor Pereira.
headlines.shortLinkText: Pressure grows on Pereira as Fulham beat Wolves

headlines.description: Amad Diallo's delightful volley stopped Ruben Amorim's first anniversary as Manchester United manager from ending in defeat and extended Nottingham Forest's wait for a second Premier League win of the season.
headlines.shortLinkText: Late Amad volley sees Utd rescue draw at Forest

headlines.description: João Pedro struck in the 34th minute to help Chelsea inflict more pain on toothless Tottenham with a dominant 1-0 victory to move up to fourth in the Premier League.
headlines.shortLinkText: Chelsea into third after win over toothless Spurs

headlines.description: Salah and Gravenberch see Liverpool snap losing streak
headlines.shortLinkText: Salah nets 250th Liverpool goal to halt losing run

name: Newcastle United at West Ham United

headlines.description: West Ham won their first home game since February, and their first under new boss Nuno Espirito Santo, by beating travel sick Newcastle 3-1.
headlines.shortLinkText: Paquetá stars in first West Ham win under Nuno

headlines.description: Erling Haaland struck a brace as Manchester City beat Bournemouth 3-1 to go second in the Premier League.
headlines.shortLinkText: Haaland scores two as City ease past Bournemouth

headlines.description: Captain Granit Xhaka's second-half equaliser earned Sunderland a point in a 1-1 draw against Everton.
headlines.shortLinkText: Xhaka levels vs. Everton as Sunderland go top 4

headlines.description: Matthijs de Ligt's 96th-minute equaliser ruled out what had seemed to be a late winnner from Richarlison as Tottenham and Man United drew 2-2.
headlines.shortLinkText: De Ligt earns Utd dramatic late draw at Spurs

headlines.description: Idrissa Gueye and Michael Keane supplemented Everton's misfiring attack in a 2-0 win which extended Fulham's woes on the road to a fifth successive match.
headlines.shortLinkText: Gueye and Keane strike for Everton to deny Fulham

headlines.description: West Ham secured their second Premier League win in a row as they overcame Burnley in east London on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: West Ham revival continues with win over Burnley

headlines.description: Sunderland claimed a stoppage-time equaliser through Brian Brobbey to deny Arsenal on Saturday at the Stadium of Light.
headlines.shortLinkText: Sunderland grab late equaliser to stun Arsenal

headlines.description: Chelsea climbed to second in the Premier League table as three second-half goals saw them to a comfortable 3-0 win over manager-less Wolves at Stamford Bridge.
headlines.shortLinkText: Chelsea cruise past Wolves to go second in Prem

headlines.description: Aston Villa cruised to a fourth straight home Premier League victory brushing Bournemouth aside 4-0 at Villa Park..
headlines.shortLinkText: Four-goal Aston Villa cruise past Bournemouth

headlines.description: Fantasy Premier League favourite Igor Thiago scored a brace in west London as Brenford earned a 3-1 vicory over 10-man Newcastle on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Igor Thiago stars as Brentford beat 10-man Toon

name: Brighton & Hove Albion at Crystal Palace

headlines.description: Crystal Palace and Brighton played out a forgettable 0-0 draw at Selhurst Park to remain a point apart in mid-table in the Premier League.
headlines.shortLinkText: Palace, Brighton share points in drab M23 derby

headlines.description: Morgan Gibbs-White produced the perfect response to his England omission by helping Nottingham Forest to their first Premier League win since the opening day of the season.
headlines.shortLinkText: Forest's Gibbs-White and Anderson deny Leeds

headlines.description: Manchester City ensured Pep Guardiola's 1,000th game in management was one to remember as they swept aside Arne Slot's lackluster Liverpool team.
headlines.shortLinkText: Haaland, Doku pile more PL misery on Liverpool

headlines.description: Pedro Neto and Enzo Fernandez scored in each half to ease Chelsea to a 2-0 win at Burnley.
headlines.shortLinkText: Pedro Neto and Enzo Fernandez fire Chelsea past Burnley

name: West Ham United at AFC Bournemouth

headlines.description: Enes Ünal scored moments after climbing off the bench to snatch a 2-2 draw for Bournemouth at home to West Ham.
headlines.shortLinkText: Late Bournemouth equaliser denies Wilson's West Ham

headlines.description: Bart Verbruggen was the hero for Brighton after his stoppage-time penalty save from Igor Thiago earned a dramatic 2-1 home victory over Brentford.
headlines.shortLinkText: Verbruggen saves late penalty as Brighton fight back to beat Brentford

headlines.description: Raul Jimenez scored the only goal as Fulham beat Sunderland 1-0 on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Jimenez has the final say as Fulham edge Sunderland

headlines.description: Liverpool's Premier League crisis deepened on Saturday with a shock 3-0 defeat at home to relegation-threatened Nottingham Forest.
headlines.shortLinkText: Liverpool slump to shock 3-0 defeat to Nottingham Forest

headlines.description: Rob Edwards suffered a 2-0 defeat to Crystal Palace in his first game in charge of Wolves via second-half goals from Daniel Muñoz and Yeremy Pino.
headlines.shortLinkText: Muñoz and Pino goals see Palace ruin Edwards Wolves homecoming

headlines.description: Newcastle brilliantly beat Manchester City 2-1 on Saturday after two goals from Harvey Barnes.
headlines.shortLinkText: Newcastle stun Man City with Barnes' brace

headlines.description: Morgan Rogers' second-half double sealed Aston Villa a 2-1 win at Leeds and lifted his side into the Premier League's top four.
headlines.shortLinkText: Rogers strike twice for Villa to deny Leeds

headlines.description: A hat-trick by Eberechi Eze earned Arsenal more bragging rights over Tottenham with a 4-1 victory.
headlines.shortLinkText: Eberechi Eze hat-trick leads Arsenal past Tottenham in north London derby

headlines.description: Idrissa Gana Gueye's astonishing sending off for slapping team-mate Michael Keane could not stop Everton from going on to secure a memorable win at Manchester United as Kiernan Dewsbury-Hall ended David Moyes' Old Trafford hoodoo.
headlines.shortLinkText: Everton beat Man Utd despite bizarre Gueye red

headlines.description: Igor Thiago scored twice in the last 10 minutes to help Brentford beat relegation-threatened Burnley 3-1
headlines.shortLinkText: Thiago brace earns Brentford win over Burnley after late drama

headlines.description: Phil Foden scored a stoppage-time winner as Manchester City killed off a Leeds fightback.
headlines.shortLinkText: Phil Foden winner helps Man City edge Leeds in Premier League

headlines.description: Brian Brobbey came off the bench to snatch Sunderland victory as they fought back from two goals down to beat Bournemouth 3-2.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brobbey secures dramatic home win for Sunderland

headlines.description: Malick Thiaw scored the fastest goal of the season after just 55 seconds as Newcastle coasted to their first Premier League away win since April with a 4-1 victory at Everton.
headlines.shortLinkText: Newcastle thrash Everton to get 1st away win

headlines.description: Thomas Frank's Tottenham tenure suffered another setback as Fulham inflicted more home woe with a 2-1 win.
headlines.shortLinkText: Spurs lose again at home as Fulham turn up heat on Thomas Frank

headlines.description: Joshua Zirkzee and Mason Mount struck in the second half as Manchester United came from behind to beat Crystal Palace 2-1 in the Premier League on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Mason Mount, Joshua Zirkzee inspire Man United comeback win at Crystal Palace

headlines.description: Boubacar Kamara's stunning goal was enough to earn Aston Villa their seventh win in eight Premier League games and bragging rights over local rivals Wolves.
headlines.shortLinkText: Boubacar Kamara nets screamer as Aston Villa beat Wolves 1-0

name: Brighton & Hove Albion at Nottingham Forest

headlines.description: Goals in each half from Maxim De Cuyper and Stefanos Tzimas earned Brighton a hard-fought 2-0 victory over Nottingham Forest at the City Ground.
headlines.shortLinkText: De Cuyper, Tzimas earn Brighton hard-fought win over Forest

headlines.description: Alexander Isak scored his first Premier League goal as Liverpool secured a much-needed 2-0 win at West Ham on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Alexander Isak nets first PL goal for Liverpool in crucial win at West Ham

headlines.description: Chelsea earned a battling 1-1 draw with Premier League leaders Arsenal at Stamford Bridge despite playing for almost an hour with 10 men.
headlines.shortLinkText: 10-man Chelsea deny Arsenal in fiery 1-1 draw between PL rivals

headlines.description: Jack Grealish's late second-half goal was enough to lead Everton to a third win in four Premier League games as the Toffees beat stuttering Bournemouth 1-0.
headlines.shortLinkText: Grealish guides Everton to win, top half of table

headlines.description: Erling Haaland brought up his century of Premier League goals as Manchester City survived a remarkable Fulham comeback in a madcap 5-4 win.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man City hold off Fulham rally in nine-goal thriller

headlines.description: Cristian Romero salvaged a late 2-2 draw for Tottenham against Newcastle at St James' Park.
headlines.shortLinkText: Romero spectacular double rescues Tottenham

headlines.description: Mikel Merino and Bukayo Saka were on target as Arsenal kept the initiative in the Premier League title race with a routine 2-0 win over Brentford.
headlines.shortLinkText: Saka goal helps Arsenal stay clear in PL title race

name: Aston Villa at Brighton & Hove Albion

headlines.description: Ollie Watkins ended an 11-game goal drought with a first-half brace as high-flying Aston Villa overturned a two-goal deficit to beat Brighton 4-3 at the Amex Stadium.
headlines.shortLinkText: Aston Villa rally to edge Brighton in 7-goal thriller

headlines.description: Daniel Munoz's first-half header was enough for Crystal Palace to leave Turf Moor with a 1-0 win as Burnley slumped to a fifth straight Premier League loss.
headlines.shortLinkText: Palace down Burnley to stay on heels of top-four sides

headlines.description: Igor Jesus scored his first Premier League goal for Nottingham Forest as they inflicted an eighth successive defeat on a doomed Wolves.
headlines.shortLinkText: Hapless Wolves beaten by Forest, stay winless

headlines.description: Leeds United produced their best display of the season in a stunning 3-1 home win against Chelsea to climb out of the Premier League's bottom three.
headlines.shortLinkText: Chelsea stunned by Leeds to dent title hopes

headlines.description: Florian Wirtz waltzed past a couple of defenders before firing home via a deflection nine minutes from time to secure Liverpool a 1-1 draw with Sunderland and spare head coach Arne Slot more scrutiny.
headlines.shortLinkText: Sunderland stay ahead of Liverpool in Anfield draw

name: West Ham United at Manchester United

headlines.description: Manchester United blew the chance to move up to fifth in the Premier League on Thursday when West Ham United salvaged a late 1-1 draw at Old Trafford.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man United lose lead late in draw with West Ham

headlines.description: Emiliano Buendía scored the last-minute winner as Aston Villa beat Arsenal 2-1 on Saturday to blow the Premier League title race wide open.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal suffer title blow after last-gasp Villa loss

headlines.description: Chelsea failed to significantly cut the gap at the top of the Premier League as they were held to a goalless draw by Bournemouth at the Vitality Stadium.
headlines.shortLinkText: Chelsea frustrated by goalless Bournemouth draw

headlines.description: Thierno Barry ended his long wait for his first goal as Everton moved to within a point of the top four with a 3-0 victory over Nottingham Forest.
headlines.shortLinkText: Thierno Barry off the mark as Everton beat Nottingham Forest

headlines.description: Manchester City cut Arsenal's lead at the top of the Premier League to two points with a comfortable 3-0 win over Sunderland on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Manchester City reduce title deficit after Sunderland victory

headlines.description: Bruno Guimarães and Anthony Gordon scored for the second time in five days as Newcastle eased to a 2-1 Premier League victory over 10-man Burnley at St James' Park.
headlines.shortLinkText: Newcastle brush 10-man Burnley aside

headlines.description: An eye-catching display by Xavi Simons inspired Tottenham to a first Premier League home win since August with a much-needed 2-0 win over Brentford.
headlines.shortLinkText: Xavi Simons scores as Tottenham earn rare home win against Brentford

headlines.description: Leeds United's Ao Tanaka scored a stoppage-time equaliser to deny Liverpool and rescue a 3-3 draw on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Leeds strike late to deny Liverpool in six-goal thriller

name: West Ham United at Brighton & Hove Albion

headlines.description: A last-gasp Georginio Rutter strike secured draw for Brighton after Jarrod Bowen's moment of inspired improvisation so nearly fired West Ham to a rare win at their bogey side.
headlines.shortLinkText: Last-gasp Rutter gets Brighton point vs. West Ham

headlines.description: Crystal Palace moved into the top four after seeing off Fulham at Craven Cottage.
headlines.shortLinkText: Palace into top four after late winner against Fulham

headlines.description: Bruno Fernandes' brace helped Manchester United get back to winning ways as rock-bottom Wolves' wretched campaign continued with a 4-1 loss on a night of protest at Molineux.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man United up to 6th with win over woeful Wolves

headlines.description: Cole Palmer scored on his first Stamford Bridge start in almost four months as Chelsea beat Everton 2-0 to move back into the Premier League's top four.
headlines.shortLinkText: Cole Palmer on scoresheet as Chelsea beat Everton

headlines.description: Hugo Ekitike scored both goals -- including the fastest of the Premier League season -- as Mohamed Salah made an earlier-than-expected return in Liverpool's 2-0 victory over Brighton at Anfield.
headlines.shortLinkText: Hugo Ektike doubles helps Liverpool win on Mohamed Salah's return

headlines.description: Harry Wilson scored one and set up two more as Fulham piled more pressure on Scott Parker by inflicting a seventh straight defeat on Burnley.
headlines.shortLinkText: Fulham inflict Burnley's seventh straight Premier League defeat

headlines.description: Arsenal were rescued by two own-goals, the second of which came in stoppage time, to beat last-placed Wolverhampton Wanderers2-1 and open up a five-point lead at the top of the Premier League.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal rescued by 2 own goals in dramatic win

headlines.description: A brace from Erling Haaland helped Manchester City beat Crystal Palace 3-0.
headlines.shortLinkText: Erling Haaland scores twice as Manchester City sink Palace at Selhurst Park

headlines.description: Tottenham goalkeeper Guglielmo Vicario had an afternoon to forget as his side suffered a dismal 3-0 defeat at Nottingham Forest.
headlines.shortLinkText: Nottingham Forest outclass Thomas Frank's lacklustre Tottenham

headlines.description: Nick Woltemade's own goal earned Sunderland the Wear-Tyne derby spoils with a 1-0 win over Newcastle.
headlines.shortLinkText: Nick Woltemade own goal sees Sunderland edge Newcastle

name: Aston Villa at West Ham United

headlines.description: Morgan Rogers scored a brace to keep Aston Villa firmly in the Premier League title chase with a 3-2 comeback win at West Ham.
headlines.shortLinkText: Rogers brace sees Villa extend winning run to nine in a row

headlines.description: Dominic Calvert-Lewin scored for a fourth-consecutive match to end Leeds' away-day blues with a 1-1 Premier League draw at Brentford.
headlines.shortLinkText: Dominic Calvert-Lewin goal helps Leeds rescue point at Brentford

headlines.description: Manchester United and Bournemouth fought out a jaw-dropping 4-4 draw as substitute Junior Kroupi struck late in one of Old Trafford's most chaotic matches ever.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man United, Bournemouth draw in see-saw thriller

headlines.description: João Pedro salvaged an exciting 2-2 draw for Chelsea against Newcastle United at St. James' Park on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: João Pedro rescues exciting Chelsea draw at Newcastle

headlines.description: Super-sub Armando Broja scored a last-minute equaliser to end Burnley's seven-game losing run and leave Bournemouth winless in eight after a 1-1 draw.
headlines.shortLinkText: Broja equalises late at Bournemouth to end Burnley losing run

headlines.description: Sunderland's impressive season following promotion to the Premier League continued with a battling goalless draw at Brighton.
headlines.shortLinkText: Sunderland battle to goalless draw at Brighton

name: West Ham United at Manchester City

headlines.description: Erling Haaland scored twice as Manchester City beat West Ham United 3-0 on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Erling Haaland scores twice as Man City cruise past West Ham

headlines.description: Wolves were beaten again as Brentford ran out 2-0 winners at Molineux on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Wolves lose again as Keane Lewis-Potter inspires Brentford

headlines.description: Second-half goals by Alexander Isak and Hugo Ekitike helped Arne Slot's Liverpool to a third consecutive victory with a dramatic 2-1 win over nine-man Tottenham.
headlines.shortLinkText: Isak and Ekitike lead Liverpool past nine-man Tottenham

headlines.description: Long-time Premier League leaders Arsenal passed the first real test of their title credentials but they were indebted to a moment of madness from Everton's Jake O'Brien.
headlines.shortLinkText: Gyökeres ends goal drought in win over Everton

headlines.description: Dominic Calvert-Lewin scored twice as Leeds United thumped Crystal Palace 4-1 at Elland Road to climb six points clear of the Premier League relegation zone.
headlines.shortLinkText: Calvert-Lewin brace helps Leeds thump Palace

headlines.description: Morgan Rogers' brilliant brace helped Aston Villa secure a 10th-straight win in all competitions as the Premier League title contenders triumphed 2-1 against Manchester United.
headlines.shortLinkText: Morgan Rogers stars with brace as Aston Villa beat Man United

headlines.description: A Raúl Jiménez penalty fired Fulham to a 1-0 victory over Nottingham Forest to condemn Sean Dyche's side to Christmas in the relegation mix.
headlines.shortLinkText: Jimenéz penalty sees Fulham past Nottingham Forest

headlines.description: Patrick Dorgu opened his Manchester United account in spectacular fashion as the makeshift right winger's rasping volley sealed a win against Newcastle.
headlines.shortLinkText: Dorgu volley gives Man United win vs. Newcastle

headlines.description: Rayan Cherki chose a good time to end his Premier League drought as his late strike earned Manchester City a vital 2-1 win at Nottingham Forest.
headlines.shortLinkText: Rayan Cherki winner helps Man City edge Forest

headlines.description: Martin Ødegaard scored to help Arsenal beat Brighton 2-1 at the Emirates on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Ødegaard scores as Arsenal edge past Brighton

headlines.description: Kevin Schade's first hat-trick for Brentford inspired a thumping 4-1 home win over sorry Bournemouth.
headlines.shortLinkText: Schade hat-trick sees Brentford past Bournemouth

headlines.description: Burnley's struggles in front of goal were on show once again as Scott Parker's relegation-threatened side failed to register a shot on target in a poor 0-0 draw against a depleted Everton side.
headlines.shortLinkText: Burnley draw blank against a depleted Everton

headlines.description: Florian Wirtz finally scored his first goal for Liverpool as he orchestrated what turned into a less-than-comfortable 2-1 Premier League win over bottom side Wolves.
headlines.shortLinkText: Wirtz bags first Liverpool goal in win vs. Wolves

headlines.description: Raúl Jiménez deepened West Ham's relegation worries as Fulham snatched a 1-0 victory at the London Stadium.
headlines.shortLinkText: West Ham woe as Jiménez gives Fulham victory

headlines.description: Aston Villa's remarkable winning run continued as Ollie Watkins came off the bench in the second half to score twice and earn a valuable 2-1 victory over Chelsea.
headlines.shortLinkText: Watkins brace seals Villa an 11th consecutive win

headlines.description: Dominic Calvert-Lewin scored for the sixth game running to extend Leeds' unbeaten run to five games after a 1-1 draw against Sunderland at the Stadium of Light.
headlines.shortLinkText: 6 in a row! Calvert-Lewin nets again in Leeds draw

headlines.description: Archie Gray's first goal for Tottenham ensured they ended a rollercoaster 2025 with a 1-0 win at Crystal Palace to ease the pressure on Thomas Frank.
headlines.shortLinkText: Spurs ease pressure on Frank with win at Palace

headlines.description: Early goals from Joelinton and Yoane Wissa set Newcastle on their way to only their second away win of the Premier League season as they held off battling Burnley.
headlines.shortLinkText: Three-goal Newcastle battle past Burnley

headlines.description: James Garner and Thierno Barry scored as Everton sealed a 2-0 win at Nottingham Forest on Tuesday night.
headlines.shortLinkText: Garner and Barry strike as Everton edge past Forest

name: Brighton & Hove Albion at West Ham United

headlines.description: Brighton soared to eighth place in the Premier League by snapping a six-match winless run with a comfortable 2-0 success over second-bottom Burnley.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brighton comfortably beat Burnley to move into eighth place

headlines.description: Arsenal scored four times in a rampant second-half display to secure a statement 4-1 win over Premier League title rivals Aston Villa at the Emirates.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal run riot in 4-1 win over Aston Villa in top-of-the-table clash

headlines.description: Manchester United's topsy-turvy 2025 ended with a poor 1-1 draw at home to rock-bottom Wolves as boss Rob Edwards collected a first point since taking charge.
headlines.shortLinkText: Winless Wolves hold Man United to a surprise draw

headlines.description: Tom Cairney's fine equaliser for Fulham saw Crystal Palace's winless streak extend to six matches in a 1-1 draw at Selhurst Park.
headlines.shortLinkText: Fulham strike late in away draw with Palace

headlines.description: Liverpool's winning run was brought to a grinding halt as Leeds claimed their first clean sheet since August with the new year bringing more frustration for Arne Slot's side.
headlines.shortLinkText: Leeds force stalemate to end Liverpool winning run

headlines.description: Thomas Frank was frustrated on his return to Brentford as Tottenham Hotspur were held to a goalless draw at the Gtech Stadium.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brentford spoil Frank's return in Tottenham draw

headlines.description: Sunderland extended their unbeaten Premier League home record to 10 games after holding Manchester City to a goalless draw.
headlines.shortLinkText: Sunderland extend home unbeaten run in City draw

headlines.description: Ollie Watkins celebrated his 250th appearance for Aston Villa with a goal in a 3-1 win against a wretched Nottingham Forest who are slipping into deep relegation trouble.
headlines.shortLinkText: Aston Villa beat Nottingham Forest to return to winning ways

name: West Ham United at Wolverhampton Wanderers

headlines.description: West Ham's relegation fears deepened as they crashed to a humiliating 3-0 defeat at previously winless Wolves.
headlines.shortLinkText: Wolves get first win as woeful West Ham's relegation fears deepen

headlines.description: Declan Rice proved why he's a leading candidate to be the Premier League's player of the season by inspiring Arsenal to a come-from behind victory at Bournemouth that opened up a six-point lead on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Rice at the double to put Arsenal 6 points clear

headlines.description: Matheus Cunha cancelled out Brenden Aaronson's strike as Ruben Amorim's Manchester United and rivals Leeds fought out a tense 1-1 draw at rocking Elland Road.
headlines.shortLinkText: Matheus Cunha equaliser earns Man Utd a draw at Leeds

headlines.description: Igor Thiago ended a six-match drought with a first Premier League hat trick in the 4-2 victory at Everton which fired Brentford to within four points of the top four.
headlines.shortLinkText: Thiago hat trick downs Everton, Brentford go 7th

headlines.description: Bruno Guimarães was Newcastle's hero as they edged past Crystal Palace to claim a second successive Premier League victory.
headlines.shortLinkText: Bruno Guimarães scores again as Newcastle edge Crystal Palace

headlines.description: Tottenham started 2026 by dropping more points at home and were booed off after Sunderland struck in the 80th minute through Brian Brobbey to rescue a 1-1 draw.
headlines.shortLinkText: Spurs booed off again as Sunderland snatch draw

headlines.description: Liverpool's hopes of finishing in the Premier League's top four received a damaging blow as Fulham's Harrison Reed struck one of the goals of the season.
headlines.shortLinkText: Harrison Reed stunner leaves Liverpool reeling as Fulham force chaotic draw

headlines.description: Enzo Fernández scored a stoppage-time equalizer as managerless Chelsea snatched a 1-1 draw at title-chasing Manchester City.
headlines.shortLinkText: Managerless Chelsea hold Man City to late draw with Enzo Fernández equalizer

name: Nottingham Forest at West Ham United

headlines.description: West Ham's Premier League survival hopes took another major blow after slipping to a 2-1 defeat at home to fellow strugglers Nottingham Forest.
headlines.shortLinkText: West Ham sink further as Morgan Gibbs-White saves Nottingham Forest

headlines.description: Antoine Semenyo scored a dramatic, long-range stoppage-time winner for Bournemouth to secure a 3-2 victory over Tottenham in his potential last match with the Cherries ahead of an expected move to Manchester City.
headlines.shortLinkText: Semenyo nets winner in likely Cherries farewell

headlines.description: Igor Thiago scored twice to keep Brentford's unlikely European push on track and become the top-scoring Brazilian in a Premier League season in a 3-0 win over Sunderland.
headlines.shortLinkText: Thiago sets Brazilian goal mark in Brentford win

headlines.description: Aston Villa's aim of making a significant dent on Arsenal's lead at the Premier League summit was thwarted by a goalless draw with Crystal Palace at Selhurst Park.
headlines.shortLinkText: Aston Villa held to goalless draw at Crystal Palace

headlines.description: Everton shot themselves in the foot as they had Michael Keane and Jack Grealish sent off in a 1-1 draw which maintained Wolves' recent momentum.
headlines.shortLinkText: Keane, Grealish see red in Everton-Wolves draw

headlines.description: Incoming head coach Liam Rosenior watched from the stands as 10-man Chelsea lost 2-1 to rivals Fulham at Craven Cottage.
headlines.shortLinkText: Rosenior watches on as Chelsea lose to Fulham

name: Brighton & Hove Albion at Manchester City

headlines.description: Title-chasing Manchester City dropped points for a third successive game as they were held to a 1-1 draw by Brighton on Wednesday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man City drop more points in draw with Brighton

headlines.description: Manchester United began life after Ruben Amorim with a 2-2 draw at struggling Burnley as substitute Jaidon Anthony's strike denied Darren Fletcher victory in his first, and perhaps only, match as interim boss.
headlines.shortLinkText: United draw at lowly Burnley after Amorim exit

headlines.description: Harvey Barnes snatched a 4-3 victory over Leeds as Newcastle served up a seven-goal Premier League thriller of which Kevin Keegan would be proud.
headlines.shortLinkText: Newcastle strike late to edge Leeds in thriller

headlines.description: Arsenal failed to extend their Premier League lead over title rivals Manchester City to eight points following a 0-0 draw against Liverpool at the Emirates.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal inch 6 pts clear with Liverpool stalemate

headlines.description: Michael Carrick enjoyed a dream start to life in the Manchester United hot seat as his team secured a morale-boosting derby victory against Manchester City.
headlines.shortLinkText: Michael Carrick masterminds Man United derby win over Man City

headlines.description: Liam Rosenior tasted victory in his first Premier League game in charge of Chelsea as goals from João Pedro and Cole Palmer earned a hard-fought 2-0 win over Brentford at Stamford Bridge.
headlines.shortLinkText: Cole Palmer scores as Liam Rosenior wins first Chelsea PL game

headlines.description: Lukas Nmecha scored the winner off the bench as Leeds UNited beat Fulham 1-0.
headlines.shortLinkText: Lukas Nmecha winner sinks Fulham as Leeds pay tribute to Terry Yorath

headlines.description: Liverpool were held to a frustrating 1-1 draw at home to Burnley on Saturday despite having 32 shots on goal.
headlines.shortLinkText: 32 shots, 1 goal as Liverpool held by lowly Burnley

headlines.description: Crystal Palace's winless run extended to 10 games in all competitions after they were beaten 2-1 by Sunderland at the Stadium of Light.
headlines.shortLinkText: Turbulent week for Crystal Palace ends in defeat at Sunderland

name: West Ham United at Tottenham Hotspur

headlines.description: Tottenham fans turned on Thomas Frank and chanted "you're getting sacked in the morning" after Callum Wilson struck a stoppage-time winner for relegation-threatened West Ham.
headlines.shortLinkText: Thomas Frank's Tottenham misery continues with home loss to West Ham

headlines.description: Arsenal missed the chance to go nine points clear at the top of the Premier League after being held to a 0-0 draw by Nottingham Forest on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal held by Nottingham Forest, fail to capitalise on Man City slip

headlines.description: Wolves extended their unbeaten run in the Premier League to four matches after they battled to a goalless draw with Newcastle at Molineux.
headlines.shortLinkText: Wolves maintain improved form in goalless draw with Newcastle

headlines.description: Aston Villa's Premier League title ambitions suffered a damaging blow as an 11-game home winning streak ended with a 1-0 defeat to Everton.
headlines.shortLinkText: Villa slip vs. Everton in chase to close Arsenal gap

name: AFC Bournemouth at Brighton & Hove Albion

headlines.description: Substitute Charalampos Kostoulas scored a stunning bicycle kick in added time as Brighton salvaged a 1-1 draw from a controversial Premier League clash with Bournemouth.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brighton's Kostoulas scores bicycle kick to salvage draw against Bournemouth

headlines.description: West Ham breathed some more life into their Premier League survival bid with a rousing 3-1 win over Sunderland.
headlines.shortLinkText: West Ham boost survival hopes by beating Sunderland

headlines.description: Cristian Romero's last-minute header rescued Tottenham a point in a 2-2 draw at Burnley but the Londoners' winless Premier League run in 2026 was extended to five matches.
headlines.shortLinkText: Romero hits late equaliser to spare Tottenham blushes against Burnley

headlines.description: Harry Wilson struck in the 93rd minute to hand Fulham a dramatic late victory over Brighton & Hove Albion.
headlines.shortLinkText: Harry Wilson's late free-kick sees Fulham sink Brighton

headlines.description: Erling Haaland had to settle for a substitute's role as Manchester City returned to winning ways with a comfortable 2-0 victory over relegation-threatened Wolves.
headlines.shortLinkText: Haaland rested as Marmoush, Semenyo see City cruise past Wolves

headlines.description: Amine Adli scrambled home a winner as Liverpool's dismal title defense suffered another setback with a dramatic 3-2 defeat at Bournemouth.
headlines.shortLinkText: Bournemouth snatch last-gasp win to stun Liverpool

headlines.description: Nottingham Forest struck twice either side off half-time to see off Brentford on Sunday afternoon.
headlines.shortLinkText: Two-goal Nottingham Forest upset Brentford

headlines.description: Estêvão opened the scoring and added an assist as Chelsea secured a 3-1 victory over 10-man Crystal Palace.
headlines.shortLinkText: Estêvão on scoresheet as Chelsea brush Palace aside

headlines.description: Emi Buendia's sweet strike set Aston Villa on their way to a hard-fought 2-0 Premier League win at Newcastle.
headlines.shortLinkText: Aston Villa dream alive after beating Newcastle

headlines.description: Matheus Cunha struck in the 88th minute to hand Manchester United a sensational victory which dents Arsenal's title hopes.
headlines.shortLinkText: Matheus Cunha and Manchester United dent Arsenal's title hopes

headlines.description: Everton striker Thierno Barry continued to justify manager David Moyes' faith by equalising in the 1-1 draw at home to Leeds United to prevent a mutiny at Hill Dickinson Stadium.
headlines.shortLinkText: Everton draw with Leeds on stellar Barry finish

headlines.description: Beto scored a late goal as Brighton and Everton shared the points at the Amex.
headlines.shortLinkText: Beto rescues point for Everton with last-gasp equaliser against Brighton

headlines.description: Arsenal have reinstated their seven-point lead at the top of the Premier League after a 4-0 win against Leeds United.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal thrash Leeds to reinstate 7-point PL lead

headlines.description: Junior Kroupi scored a stunner as AFC Bournemouth made it back-to-back wins by beating Wolves 2-0 on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Kroupi nets stunner as Bournemouth beat Wolves

headlines.description: Chelsea came from two goals down to seal a remarkable 3-2 victory over relegation-threatened West Ham United on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: 0-2 at HT, 3-2 at FT! Chelsea in epic comeback win

headlines.description: Hugo Ekitike scored two goals in just as many minutes as Liverpool got back to Premier League winning ways at Anfield against Newcastle.
headlines.shortLinkText: Ekitike at the double as Liverpool enjoy 4-1 win over Newcastle at Anfield

headlines.description: Aston Villa were controversially denied a goal by VAR as their Premier League title hopes suffered a major blow in a 1-0 home defeat to 10-man Brentford.
headlines.shortLinkText: Villa handed title blow, lose to 10-man Brentford

headlines.description: Benjamin Sesko scored a last-minute winner as Manchester United overcame Fulham in a five-goal thriller at Old Trafford on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Sesko seals late Man United win in 5-goal thriller

headlines.description: Crystal Palace were forced to settle for a 1-1 draw with Nottingham Forest who played half the game with 10 men after Neco Williams was shown a red card.
headlines.shortLinkText: Crystal Palace held by 10-man Nottingham Forest

headlines.description: A rip-roaring second-half from Tottenham saw them fight back from two goals down to claim a 2-2 draw at home to Manchester City and boost fierce rivals Arsenal's title bid.
headlines.shortLinkText: Spurs fight back vs City, Arsenal 6 clear at top

headlines.description: Habib Diarra inspired Sunderland to a comfortable 3-0 win over Burnley as they extended their unbeaten Premier League home run on Monday night.
headlines.shortLinkText: Talbi stunner caps off dominant Sunderland 3-0 win against Burnley

headlines.description: Dominic Calvert-Lewin scored his 10th Premier League goal of the season as Leeds beat Nottingham Forest 3-1 to climb nine points clear of the relegation zone.
headlines.shortLinkText: Leeds brush aside Forest to ease relegation fears

headlines.description: Manchester United's revival under Michael Carrick rolled on against Tottenham with goals from Bryan Mbeumo and Bruno Fernandes piling more misery on Thomas Frank's Spurs.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man Utd beat 10-man Spurs for 4th straight win

headlines.description: New Bournemouth signing Rayan opens account to earn home point vs. Aston Villa
headlines.shortLinkText: Villa lose ground after 1-1 draw at Bournemouth

headlines.description: Viktor Gyökeres scored a second-half brace as Arsenal piled the pressure on rivals Manchester City with a statement 3-0 win over Sunderland.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal move 9 points clear with Sunderland win

headlines.description: Burnley's woes continue as West Ham secure important victory
headlines.shortLinkText: West Ham secure vital victory as Burnley woes go on

headlines.description: A late turnaround helped Everton continue their fine away form with a 2-1 Premier League win at Fulham.
headlines.shortLinkText: Everton move into 7th with late win over Fulham

headlines.description: Cole Palmer struck a first-half hat trick as Chelsea beat Wolves 3-1 at Molineux to make it four Premier League wins from four under Liam Rosenior.
headlines.shortLinkText: Palmer hat trick gives Chelsea win over Wolves

headlines.description: Dango Ouattara blasted Brentford victory at Newcastle to boost their hopes of European qualification and further dent those of the Magpies.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brentford strike late to beat struggling Newcastle

name: Crystal Palace at Brighton & Hove Albion

headlines.description: Ismaila Sarr struck a second-half winner as Crystal Palace halted a miserable 12-match winless run with a 1-0 win away to bitter rivals Brighton.
headlines.shortLinkText: Sarr strike helps Palace beat rivals Brighton

headlines.description: Erling Haaland kept Manchester City in the title race with an added-time penalty as Pep Guardiola's side came from behind to win 2-1 in a chaotic finish.
headlines.shortLinkText: Haaland earns City dramatic late win at Liverpool

headlines.description: Chelsea's promising start to life under Liam Rosenior was derailed as Leeds fought from two down to earn a 2-2 draw at Stamford Bridge and deny the hosts a fifth straight Premier League win.
headlines.shortLinkText: Leeds mount comeback to take point at Chelsea

headlines.description: Everton's wait for the Hill Dickinson Stadium to become as imposing as Goodison Park once was continues as they again shot themselves in the foot at their new home in a 2-1 defeat to Bournemouth.
headlines.shortLinkText: Wasteful Everton suffer home loss to Bournemouth

headlines.description: Jacob Ramsey fired Newcastle United to a precious 2-1 Premier League win to pile the pressure on Thomas Frank and increase Tottenham Hotspur 's relegation fears.
headlines.shortLinkText: Newcastle win at Spurs to pile pressure on Frank

name: Manchester United at West Ham United

headlines.description: Benjamin Sesko scored in the sixth minute of stoppage time to rescue a point for Manchester United, whose winning run under Michael Carrick came to an end with a 1-1 draw at West Ham.
headlines.shortLinkText: Late Sesko goal rescues United point at West Ham

name: Brighton & Hove Albion at Aston Villa

headlines.description: Jack Hinshelwood scored a late own goal which handed Aston Villa a scrappy 1-0 Premier League victory over Brighton.
headlines.shortLinkText: Villa take 3 huge points after late Brighton OG

headlines.description: Erling Haaland moved joint-fourth on Manchester City's all-time scorer's list as Pep Guardiola's side overpowered Fulham 3-0 to turn up the heat on Arsenal.
headlines.shortLinkText: Haaland scores, Man City win to cut gap to Arsenal

headlines.description: Nottingham Forest boss Sean Dyche came under further pressure after his side were held to a 0-0 draw against rock-bottom Wolves.
headlines.shortLinkText: Forest stay 17th in draw with bottom-side Wolves

headlines.description: Burnley shocked Crystal Palace with a 3-2 comeback victory to end a 16-game Premier League winless streak and spoil Jorgen Strand Larsen's Selhurst Park debut.
headlines.shortLinkText: Burnley upset Palace to end long winless streak

headlines.description: Virgil van Dijk 's second-half header saw Liverpool end Sunderland's unbeaten Premier League run at home with a 1-0 win at the Stadium of Light.
headlines.shortLinkText: Liverpool hand Sunderland 1st Prem home loss

headlines.description: Arsenal extended their lead at the Premier League to four points after they were held at Brentford on Thursday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal drop title race points in Brentford draw

headlines.description: Debutant Tom Edozie scored a dramatic last-gasp equaliser as Wolves came from two goals down to snatch a 2-2 draw with Arsenal and plunge the Gunners' Premier League title hopes into further doubt.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal suffer title blow with dramatic Wolves draw

headlines.description: Tammy Abraham netted his first Premier League goal since returning to Aston Villa to rescue a late 1-1 draw against Leeds at Villa Park.
headlines.shortLinkText: Abraham scores late, Villa rescue points vs. Leeds

headlines.description: Brighton marked James Milner's record-breaking 654th Premier League appearance with a much-needed 2-0 win at Brentford.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brighton beat Brentford as Milner sets PL record

headlines.description: Chelsea's 10 men were stunned by a Zian Flemming header in the third minute of added time as Burnley stole a 1-1 draw at Stamford Bridge.
headlines.shortLinkText: Fofana sees red as Chelsea draw with Burnley

name: AFC Bournemouth at West Ham United

headlines.description: West Ham United missed the chance to give their survival hopes a huge shot in the arm after a goalless draw with Bournemouth.
headlines.shortLinkText: West Ham survival hopes dented vs. Bournemouth

headlines.description: Nico O'Reilly scored twice in the first half as Manchester City cut Arsenal's Premier League lead to two points with a nervy 2-1 win over Newcastle.
headlines.shortLinkText: City beat Newcastle, go within 2 points of Arsenal

headlines.description: Evan Guessand's late strike gives Crystal Palace win vs. 10-man Wolves
headlines.shortLinkText: Guessand scores late, Palace beat 10-man Wolves

headlines.description: Liverpool survived one of their worst performances of the season to snatch a last-gasp 1-0 win in a dramatic ending at Nottingham Forest.
headlines.shortLinkText: Late goal gives Liverpool dramatic win at Forest

headlines.description: Raul Jimenez scored twice as Fulham ended a three-match losing Premier League run with a 3-1 triumph at Sunderland.
headlines.shortLinkText: Jimenez bags brace as Fulham win at Sunderland

headlines.description: Eberechi Eze produced a timely brace to haunt Tottenham again and restore Arsenal's five-point lead at the Premier League summit with a 4-1 north London derby win.
headlines.shortLinkText: Eze helps Arsenal settle title nerves with NLD win

headlines.description: Manchester United striker Benjamin Sesko came to Michael Carrick's rescue for the third time in four matches after arriving from the bench to score the only goal in the 1-0 victory at Everton.
headlines.shortLinkText: Sesko sends Man United past Everton into top 4

headlines.description: Wolves moved past Derby's record-low Premier League points tally as they dealt a blow to Aston Villa's top-four hopes with a 2-0 win at Molineux.
headlines.shortLinkText: Wolves beat Villa in derby for rare PL win

headlines.description: Substitute Evanilson claimed a second-half equaliser as Bournemouth stretched their unbeaten run to eight Premier League games by battling back to draw 1-1 against Sunderland.
headlines.shortLinkText: Evanilson rescues Bournemouth point, 8 unbeaten

headlines.description: Burnley's spirited fightback counted for nothing as Mikkel Damsgaard struck in injury time to earn Brentford a dramatic 4-3 victory at Turf Moor.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brentford beat Burnley in incredible 7-goal thriller

headlines.description: Liverpool boosted their hopes of Champions League qualification after beating West Ham United 5-2 at Anfield on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Liverpool boost UCL hopes with 5-2 West Ham win

headlines.description: Thierno Barry came off the bench to fire Everton to Premier League victory at Newcastle for the second successive season in a dramatic conclusion to a madcap encounter.
headlines.shortLinkText: Barry fires Everton to dramatic win at Newcastle

headlines.description: Antoine Semenyo netted the only goal of the game as Manchester City beat Leeds United 1-0 on Saturday to cut Arsenal's Premier League lead to two points.
headlines.shortLinkText: Semenyo scores only goal as City cut Arsenal lead

name: Nottingham Forest at Brighton & Hove Albion

headlines.description: Evergreen Danny Welbeck felled Nottingham Forest with his 10th Premier League goal of the season firing Brighton to a 2-1 victory.
headlines.shortLinkText: Welbeck fires Brighton past strugglers Forest

headlines.description: Tottenham's relegation fears increased after a 2-1 loss at Fulham consigned them to a fourth consecutive Premier League defeat.
headlines.shortLinkText: Spurs lose again as relegation fears deepen

headlines.description: Manchester United came from a goal down to claim another morale-boosting victory over Crystal Palace on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Sesko nets another winner in United comeback

headlines.description: Arsenal passed a major test of their Premier League title credentials after Jurriën Timber and William Saliba both scored from corners in a 2-1 win over Chelsea on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal edge past 10-man Chelsea, stretch PL lead

headlines.description: Bournemouth's wait for a first Premier League win over Brentford goes on after two European hopefuls shared a goalless draw at the Vitality Stadium.
headlines.shortLinkText: Bournemouth, Brentford share points in 0-0 draw

headlines.description: Everton won at home for the first time in three months to keep themselves in the hunt for European football with a 2-0 victory over struggling Burnley.
headlines.shortLinkText: Everton up to 8th place after win over Burnley

headlines.description: Habib Diarra's second-half penalty snatched Sunderland a 1-0 win at Elland Road and dealt Leeds' Premier League survival fight a blow.
headlines.shortLinkText: Sunderland get past Leeds, win 1st PL game of 5

headlines.description: André scored a last-gasp winner as Wolves stunned Liverpool 2-1 and earned back-to-back wins in the Premier League for the first time since April 2024.
headlines.shortLinkText: Liverpool sunk by Wolves on late deflected strike

headlines.description: João Pedro scored a hat trick as Chelsea boosted their Champions League hopes with a 4-1 win over top-five rivals Aston Villa on Wednesday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Pedro treble at Villa boosts Chelsea's UCL bid

headlines.description: Arsenal extended their lead at the top of the Premier League table to seven points after scraping a 1-0 win over Brighton at the Amex Stadium.
headlines.shortLinkText: Saka's deflected strike puts Arsenal 7 points clear

headlines.description: Crysencio Summerville continued his one-man mission to save West Ham with the goal which sank Fulham 1-0.
headlines.shortLinkText: West Ham pick up big win in battle to beat drop

headlines.description: Manchester City dropped two crucial points in the title race as relegation-threatened Nottingham Forest twice came from behind twice to rescue a 2-2 draw at the Etihad Stadium.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man City stumble in title chase with Forest draw

headlines.description: 10-man Newcastle ended Manchester United's seven-match unbeaten streak after a stunning late winner from William Osula at St. James' Park.
headlines.shortLinkText: 10-man Newcastle stun Man Utd after Osula winner

headlines.description: Tottenham's survival hopes took a turn for the worse after a nightmare 24-hour period concluded with a 3-1 home defeat to Crystal Palace following Micky van de Ven's foolish sending-off.
headlines.shortLinkText: Spurs' relegation fears worsen after Palace defeat

headlines.description: Burnley and Bournemouth shared the points in a 0-0 draw at Turf Moor on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Burnley and Bournemouth battle to a goalless draw

headlines.description: Brighton edged Sunderland 1-0 on Saturday after a winning goal from Yankuba Minteh.
headlines.shortLinkText: Minteh strikes as Brighton defeat Sunderland

headlines.description: Arsenal claimed a late 2-0 win against Everton thanks to two moments of brilliance from Max Dowman.
headlines.shortLinkText: Dowman magic inspires Arsenal against Everton

headlines.description: Chelsea's dismal week went from bad to worse on Saturday as Anthony Gordon condemned the Blues to a defeat that could prove vital in the Champions League race.
headlines.shortLinkText: Gordon strike caps off dismal week for Chelsea

name: Manchester City at West Ham United

headlines.description: Manchester City 's title bid was left in tatters after a 1-1 draw at relegation-threatened West Ham left them nine points behind leaders Arsenal.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man City hand Arsenal huge boost with costly draw

headlines.description: Leeds suffered further punishment after Dominic Calvert-Lewin missed a penalty and Gabriel Gudmundsson was sent off in a goalless draw at Crystal Palace.
headlines.shortLinkText: Palace held by 10-man Leeds as DCL misses penalty

headlines.description: Manchester United took a huge leap towards securing Champions League football next season after beating top-five rivals Aston Villa 3-1 in the Premier League on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: United take huge stride towards UCL with Villa win

headlines.description: Nottingham Forest climbed out of the Premier League relegation zone, but they will see the 0-0 draw against Fulham as a missed opportunity.
headlines.shortLinkText: Forest move out of drop zone in Fulham stalemate

headlines.description: Richarlison scored a late equaliser as Tottenham snatched a 1-1 draw at Liverpool, securing Igor Tudor his first point as interim boss.
headlines.shortLinkText: Richarlison snatches point for Spurs at Liverpool

headlines.description: Tolu Arokodare scored a late Wolverhampton Wanderers equaliser as Brentford let a two-goal lead slip to dent their Champions League challenge.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brentford's European hopes hit, draw with Wolves

headlines.description: Harry Maguire was shown a costly red card as Manchester United were forced to settle for a 2-2 Premier League draw at Bournemouth.
headlines.shortLinkText: Maguire sent off as Man Utd held by Bournemouth

headlines.description: Two goals from Danny Welbeck condemned Liverpool to a 2-1 defeat to Brighton on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Welbeck scores twice as Brighton beat Liverpool

headlines.description: Harry Wilson grabbed his 10th goal of the season as Fulham shoved their former boss Scott Parker closer to the drop with a 3-1 win over Burnley.
headlines.shortLinkText: Harry Wilson scores again as Fulham beat Burnley

headlines.description: A miserable 10 days for Chelsea culminated in a chastening 3-0 defeat as Everton won back-to-back matches at home in the Premier League for the first time this season.
headlines.shortLinkText: Beto scores two as Everton pile misery on Chelsea

headlines.description: Leeds missed the chance to climb six points clear of the Premier League's bottom three after being held to a frustrating 0-0 draw by Brentford at Elland Road.
headlines.shortLinkText: Defences on top as Leeds draw 0-0 with Brentford

headlines.description: Brian Brobbey scored a 90th-minute winner in Sunderland's 2-1 win over Newcastle in Sunday's Tyne-Wear derby.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brobbey hands Sunderland late win at Newcastle

name: West Ham United at Aston Villa

headlines.description: Ollie Watkins fired the perfect response to his England snub by helping Aston Villa to a 2-0 win over West Ham.
headlines.shortLinkText: Watkins responds to England snub with a goal as Villa beat West Ham

headlines.description: Tottenham collapsed amid a white-hot atmosphere to lose 3-0 at home to Nottingham Forest and increase fears of a seismic relegation.
headlines.shortLinkText: Forest thrash Tottenham in relegation six-pointer
